article_title: Global Venture Capital Hits $189 Billion Monthly Record as Three AI Companies Absorb 83 Percent of All Funding

content_preview.title: Global Venture Capital Hits $189 Billion Monthly Record as Three AI Companies Absorb 83 Percent of All Funding
content_preview.summary: February 2026 shattered all venture capital records with $189 billion deployed globally, but 83 percent of that capital flowed to just three AI companies, deepening a K-shaped market where mega-rounds surge while seed funding contracts.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Global venture capital investment reached $189 billion in February 2026, the largest single month of startup funding ever recorded, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/03/openai-anthropic-waymo-dominated-189-billion-vc-investments-february-crunchbase-report/). The figure represents a 780 percent increase from the $21.5 billion deployed in February 2025 and eclipses every prior monthly record by a wide margin. But the headline number conceals a structural reality...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent Analysis piece at 928 words providing comprehensive coverage of VC market concentration. Clear narrative structure from record-setting headline through mega-rounds, K-shaped dynamics, geographic concentration, and robotics breakout. Strong closing analysis connecting private and public market signals.
editor_notes.source_verification: Source 1 (techcrunch.com/Crunchbase report): Confirmed $189B total, 780% YoY from $21.5B, OpenAI $110B at $730B valuation, Anthropic $30B at $380B, Waymo $16B at $126B, 83% concentration, 90% AI share, 92% US share ($174B), seed decline 11% to $2.6B, early-stage growth 47% to $13.1B, Mind Robotics $500M, Rhoda AI $450M, Rapidus, Wayve, World Labs, Cerebras. Source 2 (techcrunch.com/AI eating venture): Confirmed 41% of $128B Carta platform funding to AI startups, K-shaped market framing. Source 3 (techcrunch.com/17 companies): Confirmed 17 US-based AI companies raised $100M+, 3 exceeding $1B (content partially truncated but key claims visible).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All numerical claims verified against TechCrunch sources: $189B total, individual round sizes and valuations for OpenAI/Anthropic/Waymo, concentration percentages, seed/early-stage splits, geographic distribution. The robotics companies and amounts are confirmed from Source 1. Highly accurate data-driven article.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. The article presents the concentration as a factual observation with appropriate context rather than editorializing about it. The 'K-shaped market' framing is attributed to analysts. The closing section raises important questions without prescribing answers.
editor_notes.originality: Novel coverage of February 2026 VC data. No prior coverage of this specific Crunchbase report found in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exceptional data journalism article. All three sources verified and all major numerical claims confirmed. Well-structured analysis that contextualizes the headline numbers with structural market dynamics. Ready for publication.
